title: Couldn't change binded file inside container by command in entrypoint
body:
/kind bug

I see that podman allow bind single file https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/5134
But inside the container, this file couldn't be changed by sed command in entrypoint.

```
podman run -v "./stend/test/inside.ini":"/root/services/inside/online/sbis-rpc-service.ini":Z -v "./stend/test/inside-ps.ini":"/root/services/inside/online-ps/sbis-rpc-service.ini":Z --rm --entrypoint sleep --name="inside" --hostname "10-176-134-209.dev-vpn.corp.tensor.ru" --net host --shm-size=1g  --memory="16g" --memory-swap="32g" dev-registry.sbis.ru/local_stand/inside:20.3100-night 5 && sed -i "s/#your_hostname/`hostname`/g" /root/services/inside/online/sbis-rpc-service.ini /root/services/inside/online-ps/sbis-rpc-service.ini
sed: can't read /root/services/inside/online/sbis-rpc-service.ini: Permission denied
sed: can't read /root/services/inside/online-ps/sbis-rpc-service.ini: Permission denied
```

**Output of `podman version`:**
```
# podman version
Version:            2.0.0-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Sorry that this issue's been neglected for so long. I see that you were running a v2.0.0-dev build; there's been a massive amount of change to master over the last month, so would it be possible to try again with the latest master?

Also, are you running Podman rootless, or as root?

> Sorry that this issue's been neglected for so long. I see that you were running a v2.0.0-dev build; there's been a massive amount of change to master over the last month, so would it be possible to try again with the latest master?

I see that since build `2.0.0-0.13.dev.git5b4e91d.fc33` broken `WORKDIR`. Any command in entrypoint that should be executed relatively WORKDIR now not working. Because start directory now `/` instead of `WORKDIR`. Last good working build is `2.0.0-0.10.dev.git8857ba2.fc32`

On build `2.0.0-0.61.dev.git6aa802d.fc33` both issues are still reproduced.

> Also, are you running Podman rootless, or as root?

I am running Podman rootless.
Can you give us a simple reproducer?
@NTMan Could you describe a simple reproducer?
I don't think anyone has managed to reproduce this. I'm going to close this for a lack of information on how to proceed; if someone has a reproducer, please comment and we can reopen.

> I don't think anyone has managed to reproduce this. I'm going to close this for a lack of information on how to proceed; if someone has a reproducer, please comment and we can reopen.

I have prepared a demo here: https://github.com/NTMan/podman-example

I just downloaded and tried podman-example


$ podman run  -v "$PWD/inside.ini":"/root/inside.ini":Z localhost/bugreport:1.0
sed: cannot rename /root/sedcxVmUe: Device or resource busy

This looks like sed is attempting to mv the /root/inside.ini file which since it is a mount point, is not allowed
So I changed the test a little and did
$ mkdir root
$ cp inside.ini root/
$ podman run  -v "$PWD/root":"/root":Z localhost/bugreport:1.0

And it succeeds.
